examples.title: Basic Pointer Operations
examples.description: Demonstrates & and * operators for obtaining and using addresses
examples.explanation: This program shows basic pointer operations: & gets the address of x and stores it in ptr, then * dereferences ptr to read or modify the value at that address.

examples.title: Pointer Arithmetic with Arrays
examples.description: Shows how pointer arithmetic works with arrays
examples.explanation: This demonstrates pointer arithmetic: adding an integer to a pointer moves it by that many elements (not bytes), array indexing p[i] is equivalent to *(p+i), and subtracting two pointers gives the number of elements between them.

examples.title: Pointer to Pointer and NULL
examples.description: Demonstrates pointer to pointer and NULL pointer usage
examples.explanation: Shows multi-level indirection: **pp accesses the value through two pointers. Also demonstrates NULL pointer initialization and comparison.

examples.title: Function with Pointers - Call by Reference
examples.description: Demonstrates passing pointers to functions to modify values
examples.explanation: Demonstrates call by reference in C: functions receive addresses of variables, dereference them to modify original values. The swap function exchanges values at addresses, doubleValue modifies x in place.
